Nigerian manufacturers, others to save $1.4b yearly on Chinese tariff removal - The Nation Newspaper Monday, March 16, 2026 Advertise With Us E-paper. March 16, 2026 by  Chikodi Okereocha. # Nigerian manufacturers, others to save $1.4b yearly on Chinese tariff removal. Manufacturers have welcomed Chinese President Xi Jinping’s decision to remove tariffs on imports from 53 African countries starting May 1, 2026, noting that the zero-tariff policy will open one of the world’s largest consumer markets to African producers and manufacturers. In the short term, resource-rich countries may see faster growth in shipments of oil, minerals, and agricultural commodities. “The zero-tariff window should serve as a catalyst for industrial upgrading, regional value chains, and stronger export competitiveness,” it said. Instead, these gains will depend on Africa’s ability to move up the value chain, producing semi-processed and finished goods that meet international quality standards. * ‘What China’s Xi told me about Taiwan’. * What Nigeria can learn from the China’s poverty eradication model. ##### Nigerian manufacturers, others to save $1.4b yearly on Chinese tariff removal 1 minute ago. https://thenationonlineng.net/nigerian-manufacturers-others-to-save-1-4b-yearly-on-chinese-tariff-removal/amp/

NSIA, Asset Green Ltd seal deal on developing dairy production platform - APAnews - Agence de Presse Africaine #### NSIA, Asset Green Ltd seal deal on developing dairy production platform. # NSIA, Asset Green Ltd seal deal on developing dairy production platform. Facebook  Twitter  Email  LinkedIn  Telegram  WhatsApp. The Nigeria Sovereign Investment Authority (NSIA) has signed a Memorandum of Understanding with UK-based Asset Green Ltd. to develop an integrated dairy production platform. The British Deputy High Commissioner to Nigeria, Jonny Baxter, said in a statement on Wednesday that the project would transform Nigeria’s dairy sector and strengthen national food security. According to him, the initiative is among Nigeria’s most ambitious dairy investments, combining 20,000 hectares of climate-smart forage production with a 10,000-milking cow operation. Baxter described the NSIA-Asset Green collaboration as a “full-circle moment” in bilateral relations and a sign of shared commitment to sustainable growth. In his remarks, the Asset Green Director, Rod Bassett, said that the partnership would unlock Nigeria’s agricultural potential. Bassett, who is also the Chief Executive Officer of Agrium Capital Ltd., said that the Greenfield investment would develop infrastructure, boost local production and cut import dependence. https://apanews.net/nsia-asset-green-ltd-seal-deal-on-developing-dairy-production-platform/

2 Vanguard Index Funds to Beat the S&P 500 Over the Next 10 Years, According to Analysts - The Motley Fool And the good news for investors is that it offers simple, inexpensive ETFs you can buy to invest in market segments poised to outperform the large-cap index. The most recent update from Vanguard's model shows two segments of the market outperforming over the long run: small-cap stocks and value stocks. With valuations as the core basis for Vanguard's model, it's easy to see why it expects small-cap and value stocks to outperform. For investors looking to tilt their portfolios toward small-cap and value stocks, Vanguard offers two simple and inexpensive ETFs to do just that. Since it uses standard market-cap weighting, it lets stocks that appreciate in value take up a larger portion of the portfolio. The **Vanguard Value ETF** (VTV +0.76%) tracks the performance of the **CRSP U.S. Large Cap Value Index**, which includes the less expensive half of the broader market.
